description:
## Summary

Add `bd reset` command that wraps `git reset` and immediately runs mismatch detection, eliminating the lazy-detection gap where users must run a separate `bd` command after `git reset` to trigger Dolt sync.

Also add `bd check-refs` as a standalone explicit ref check command.

## Motivation

Phase 1 detection runs in PersistentPreRun (lazy). Users do `git reset --hard HEAD~1` then must remember to run `bd list` or similar to trigger the sync. This is confusing and error-prone. `bd reset` makes it one step.

## Design

### `bd reset` command
- Passes all arguments through to `git reset` (e.g., `bd reset --hard HEAD~1`)
- After git reset succeeds, immediately runs `checkBeadsRefSync()`
- Respects all `branch_strategy.*` settings (prompt, auto-reset, silent)
- Exits with git's exit code if git reset fails

### `bd check-refs` command
- Standalone explicit ref check (same as what PersistentPreRun does)
- Useful for scripts, shell aliases, or manual verification
- Respects `branch_strategy.*` settings — does NOT force sync
- Exit code: 0 = in sync or action taken, 1 = mismatch detected but no action (silent mode)

### Lazy detection retained
- PersistentPreRun still runs `checkBeadsRefSync()` as fallback
- Catches cases where user does bare `git reset` without `bd reset`

### Shell integration (deferred to Phase 5)
- Shell function wrapping `git` to intercept `git reset` and run `bd check-refs`
- Installed by `bd init` with user opt-in
- See gist §14.5 for details
notes:
## Behavior Clarification (2026-03-07)

bd check-refs (and the check that runs after bd reset) follows this logic:

1. Compare .beads/refs hash against current Dolt commit hash
2. If match: do nothing (in sync)
3. If mismatch: check branch_strategy.* settings
   3a. If prompt disabled: follow the default strategy silently
       - reset_dolt_with_git=true → reset Dolt to match refs (keep histories in sync)
       - reset_dolt_with_git=false → allow git and Dolt histories to diverge
   3b. If prompt enabled: prompt user, with default strategy as the default answer
       - reset_dolt_with_git=true → default is Yes [Y/n] (reset)
       - reset_dolt_with_git=false → default is No [y/N] (allow divergence)
4. Execute the selected strategy: either reset Dolt or allow divergence

Exit codes: 0 always (all paths are intentional strategy execution). Non-zero only for errors (e.g. git reset fails).

## Remediation Items
- Fix check-refs help text (remove incorrect exit code 1 claim)
- Add tests for bd reset and bd check-refs
title: bd reset: eager git-reset wrapper with immediate ref check

description:
The feature/beads-refs branch has 87 commits including 14 merge commits from main,
~11 unrelated fixes already in main, backup noise, and a commit+revert pair. This
makes the PR (GH#2473) hard to review. Rebase to produce 4 clean squashed commits:

1. **feat: beads refs — track git-dolt history with branch_strategy config** (core impl, ~15 commits)
2. **feat: add bd reset and bd check-refs commands** (new commands, 4 commits)
3. **test: beads refs round-trip, E2E, and behavioral tests** (all tests, 7 commits)
4. **docs: CHANGELOG, config templates, and branch_strategy documentation** (docs, 5 commits)

## Plan

1. Rebase onto main (drops merge commits and already-merged fixes automatically)
2. Drop noise: 2 backup commits, 2 .beads/backup resets, 1 commit+revert pair
3. Interactive rebase to squash remaining ~35 commits into 4 groups

## Potential Complication

The rebase onto main may hit conflicts since many commits touch the same files
(hooks.go, init_templates.go, vc.go, config.go). If conflicts are extensive or
cascading, the interactive rebase becomes impractical.

## Backup Plan

- `feature/beads-refs-v2` branch preserves the original HEAD as a backup
- If rebase conflicts are unmanageable: abandon rebase, `git reset --hard feature/beads-refs-v2`
- Alternative approach: create a fresh branch from main, `git merge --squash feature/beads-refs-v2`
  to get all changes as a single diff, then selectively commit in 4 groups using `git add -p`
- Worst case: squash-merge the PR as-is (GitHub "Squash and merge" button)
notes: Parent epic: bd-vlu.1 (Phase 1: Beads Refs). Backup branch: feature/beads-refs-v2 at commit c72686bb.
title: Rebase feature/beads-refs: tidy 87 commits into 4 logical groups

description:
branch_strategy.* settings are currently stored in the Dolt config table via bd config set. This breaks on DOLT_RESET --hard because the config table is wiped along with all other Dolt state. The current workaround is .beads/sync_config (a git-tracked cache), which is duplicative.

1. IsYamlOnlyKey() in internal/config/yaml_config.go controls routing
2. SetYamlConfig() / GetYamlConfig() handle read/write to config.yaml
3. bd config set already checks IsYamlOnlyKey() and routes accordingly

This eliminates the need for .beads/sync_config entirely.

description: Epic tracking implementation of branch isolation features per Gist.BranchingStrategies.md. Three complementary features extending existing Beads infrastructure with no new daemon: (1) Per-branch merge strategy (stay-on-main / merge-with-branch / merge-on-close), (2) Cross-branch message delivery via cherry-pick, (3) BEADS_HEAD pointer file for git-dolt correspondence, (4) Protected branch merge via CI. All features are Beads-level. Target: v0.58.0+.
design:
## Data Model

### beads_branches registry table (migration 009)
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS beads_branches (
    branch_name VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY,
    merge_strategy VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'stay-on-main',
    status VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
)
```
merge_strategy: stay-on-main, merge-with-branch, merge-on-close. status: active, merged, abandoned. main is never registered.

### Strategy naming
- Canonical names (config, flags, env vars, DB): stay-on-main, merge-with-branch, merge-on-close
- Interactive shortcuts (prompt only): A, B, C
- Section labels (docs): Strategy A, Strategy B, Strategy C

### .beads/DOLT_HEAD pointer file
Format: dolt_commit: <hash> + dolt_branch: <branch>. Written after every Dolt auto-commit. Git-tracked.

### Configuration
- branch.prompt_strategy: true/false (default true) — prompt on new branch
- branch.default_strategy: stay-on-main/merge-with-branch/merge-on-close (default stay-on-main)
- BD_MERGE_STRATEGY env var: same values as config
- Resolution order: --strategy flag > BD_MERGE_STRATEGY > branch.default_strategy > stay-on-main

### Implementation Order (by phase)
Phase 1: bd-vlu.1.1 (BEADS_HEAD) → bd-vlu.1.2 (post-checkout hook)
Phase 2: bd-vlu.2.1 (registry) → bd-vlu.2.2 (strategy selection) → bd-vlu.2.3 (Strategy A) + bd-vlu.2.4 (Strategy B) → bd-vlu.2.5 (merge CI flags) → bd-vlu.2.6 (naming alignment)
Phase 3: bd-vlu.3.1 (Strategy C) → bd-vlu.3.2 (conflict resolution)
Phase 4: bd-vlu.4.1 (cross-branch messaging) → bd-vlu.4.2 (schema migration) + bd-vlu.4.3 (GitHub Action)

### Key Decisions
- Second *sql.DB connection for merge operations (Strategy C) in Dolt server mode
- Registry table on main, not per-branch
- Cherry-pick for messaging (not merge — preserves isolation)
- BEADS_HEAD is a simple text file, not JSON
- Strategy A (stay-on-main) has no Dolt branch — writes directly to main
- Declarative strategy names in all programmatic interfaces; A/B/C only for interactive prompt
title: Dolt Branch Isolation: Per-Branch Merge Strategies, Cross-Branch Messaging, and Git-Dolt History Correspondence

description:
Pointer files (.beads/HEAD + .beads/refs/heads/<branch>), mismatch detection in PersistentPreRun, branch_strategy.* config settings, and writeBeadsRefs integration into commit paths. Purely additive — records Dolt state into git-tracked files. All settings default to false — zero user-visible behavior changes.

## Revised Approach (2026-03-06)

Phase 1 pairs `git reset` with `DOLT_RESET` — both destructive operations that move the branch pointer. `git checkout` (non-destructive, detached HEAD) is deferred to Phase 2 (Strategy B) because Dolt has no native detached HEAD and supporting it requires temporary branch creation.

Mismatch detection runs in PersistentPreRun (every `bd` command), NOT in the post-checkout hook. This ensures:
1. Correct semantic pairing (reset ↔ reset, not checkout ↔ reset)
2. Reliable Dolt store context (no ensureStoreActive() workaround)
3. No false positives from git checkout

## Configuration

Four settings under branch_strategy.* namespace, all default false:
- branch_strategy.default_strategy (stay-on-main)
- branch_strategy.prompt (false — silent mode)
- branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git (false — no auto-reset)
- branch_strategy.defaults.checkout_dolt_with_git (false — Phase 2)

## Remaining Work

- **bd-vlu.1.7** (P1): Move detection from post-checkout hook to PersistentPreRun, implement branch_strategy.* settings
- **bd-vlu.1.3**: Re-run manual validation after the code change
- **bd-vlu.1.4**: Update automated tests for the new entry point and settings
- **bd-vlu.1.5**: Update CHANGELOG
- **bd-vlu.1.6**: Review contribution guidelines and submit PR

## Gate

Refs write correctly on every Dolt commit. All four branch_strategy settings combinations work correctly. git reset --hard followed by any bd command respects settings (silent by default, prompt or auto-reset when enabled). Round-trip integrity confirmed (reset backward then forward, no data loss or duplication).
title: Phase 1: Beads Refs

description:
Implement git-Dolt correspondence pointer files (gist §6). Mirror git's own ref structure:

- `.beads/HEAD` — contains `ref: refs/heads/<branch>` identifying current Dolt branch
- `.beads/refs/heads/<branch>` — contains Dolt commit hash at branch tip

After every Dolt auto-commit:
1. Write current branch name to .beads/HEAD (if changed)
2. Write DOLT_HASHOF('HEAD') to .beads/refs/heads/<current-branch>
3. git add .beads/HEAD .beads/refs/heads/<current-branch>

Read path (§6.4): post-checkout git hook reads .beads/HEAD to determine Dolt branch, compares hash in .beads/refs/heads/<branch> against current Dolt state. On mismatch, prompt: [R]eset Beads DB / [K]eep current. Reset uses DOLT_RESET('--hard', hash). Agent mode: BD_AUTO_SYNC=1 auto-selects Reset. Rebase/stash safety (§6.5): skip during rebase-in-progress, skip file-level checkouts.
title: Implement Beads refs (.beads/HEAD + .beads/refs/heads/)

description:
## Summary

Create an E2E integration test that exercises `bd reset --hard HEAD~1` against a real git repo with a real Dolt container, verifying both git and Dolt state move backward together, then forward for round-trip integrity.

## Context / Gap Analysis

**Existing coverage:**
- `TestAutoResetRoundTrip` (git_reset_test.go:386) — tests Dolt reset round-trip via `writeTestBeadsRefs` + `checkBeadsRefSync` against a real Docker Dolt container. Verifies issue creation, backward reset (issue disappears), forward reset (issue returns), and `getDoltCommitMessage` for forward-unreachable commits. **Does NOT exercise the CLI or git repo layer.**
- `TestCleanupStaleBeadsRefsInGitRepo` (git_reset_test.go:489) — tests ref file cleanup via `git rm --cached` inside a real git repo. **Does NOT exercise Dolt at all.**

**Gap:** No test exercises the full `bd reset` CLI command path (git reset → ref mismatch detection → Dolt reset) inside a real git repo with a real Dolt container.

## Technical Architecture

**Location:** `cmd/bd/git_reset_test.go` (alongside existing reset tests)

**Infrastructure used (all existing — no new infra needed):**
- `testutil.StartIsolatedDoltContainer(t)` — per-test Dolt container with auto-cleanup
- `newTestStoreWithPrefix(t, dbPath, prefix)` — creates DoltStore against test container
- `config.Initialize()` + `config.Set("branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git", true)` — enables ref sync
- `writeTestBeadsRefs(t, beadsDir, branch, hash)` — simulates what a git reset does to refs
- `checkBeadsRefSync(ctx, store)` — triggers mismatch detection and Dolt reset

**Approach:** Internal function-level test (NOT subprocess). Uses the same pattern as `TestAutoResetRoundTrip` but adds a real git repo layer:
1. Create temp git repo (`git init` + `git config`)
2. Place `.beads/` inside the git repo
3. Create issues, commit to Dolt, commit to git
4. Simulate `bd reset --hard HEAD~1` by: writing beads refs with the prior Dolt hash, then calling `checkBeadsRefSync`
5. Verify git state (via git log) and Dolt state (via store queries)

**Why not subprocess:** The `bd reset` command calls `git reset` + `checkBeadsRefSyncWithGitLine`. Testing via subprocess requires building the `bd` binary, managing PATH, and dealing with process isolation. The function-level approach tests the same logic paths with better isolation and faster execution — consistent with `TestAutoResetRoundTrip`.

## Test Plan

### Test: `TestResetRoundTripWithGitRepo`

**Setup:**
1. `t.TempDir()` → create temp dir
2. `git init` + `git config user.email/name` inside temp dir
3. Create `.beads/` dir, initialize DoltStore via `newTestStoreWithPrefix`
4. Enable branch_strategy via config
5. Initial git commit (empty or with a file)

**Phase 1 — Build two commits:**
1. Create issue A in Dolt, `store.Commit()`, record `checkpointHash`
2. Write beads refs, `git add .beads/`, `git commit -m "checkpoint"`
3. Create issue B in Dolt, `store.Commit()`, record `latestHash`
4. Write beads refs, `git add .beads/`, `git commit -m "latest"`

**Phase 2 — Backward reset:**
1. `git reset --hard HEAD~1` (moves git HEAD back)
2. Verify git log shows checkpoint commit at HEAD
3. Read `.beads/refs/heads/main` — should now have `checkpointHash` (restored by git reset)
4. Call `checkBeadsRefSync(ctx, store)` — should detect mismatch and reset Dolt
5. Verify `store.GetCurrentCommit()` == `checkpointHash`
6. Verify `store.GetIssue(ctx, issueB.ID)` returns not-found
7. Verify `store.GetIssue(ctx, issueA.ID)` succeeds

**Phase 3 — Forward reset:**
1. Write beads refs with `latestHash`, `git add .beads/`, `git commit`, OR simulate by writing refs directly
2. Call `checkBeadsRefSync(ctx, store)`
3. Verify `store.GetCurrentCommit()` == `latestHash`
4. Verify both issues A and B exist

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Test passes with Docker available (real Dolt container)
- [ ] Test skips gracefully when Docker unavailable (`skipIfNoDolt` pattern)
- [ ] Both git state and Dolt state verified at each step
- [ ] Round-trip integrity: backward then forward preserves all data
- [ ] No new test infrastructure or helpers needed

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Write the test function (~50-80 lines)

1. Add `TestResetRoundTripWithGitRepo` to `cmd/bd/git_reset_test.go`
2. Setup: temp dir, git init, git config, create .beads dir
3. Create DoltStore via `newTestStoreWithPrefix` (skips if no Dolt)
4. Enable `branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git` via config
5. Create two Dolt commits with issues, writing beads refs and git committing at each step
6. Backward reset: `git reset --hard HEAD~1`, verify git log, call `checkBeadsRefSync`, verify Dolt state
7. Forward reset: write refs with latest hash, call `checkBeadsRefSync`, verify both issues exist
8. Cleanup via t.Cleanup (config reset)

### Phase 2: Verify

1. Run test with Docker: `go test -run TestResetRoundTripWithGitRepo -v ./cmd/bd/ -count=1`
2. Run test without Docker: verify graceful skip message
3. Run full reset test suite: `go test -run TestReset -v ./cmd/bd/ -count=1`

description:
## Problem

In `cmd/bd/branch_messaging.go:84`, when `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK` fails with conflicts during cross-branch message delivery, the cleanup calls:

```go
_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_MERGE('--abort')")
```

This is wrong. `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` only aborts in-progress merges, not cherry-picks. After a failed cherry-pick, no merge is in progress, so this call is a silent no-op — leaving the target branch's working set dirty with conflict markers.

The connection close (`defer deliveryDB.Close()`) provides an accidental safety net because Dolt discards uncommitted session state on disconnect, but relying on implicit cleanup is fragile.

## Root Cause

Copy-paste from `branch_strategy.go:70` which correctly uses `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` after a failed `DOLT_MERGE()`.

## Fix

Replace with `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')`, which Dolt supports as the purpose-built cleanup:

```go
// Before (wrong):
_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_MERGE('--abort')")

// After (correct):
_, _ = db.ExecContext(ctx, "CALL DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')")
```

Per Dolt docs: DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort') "Abort the current conflict resolution process, and revert all changes from the in-process cherry-pick operation."

## Test Plan

### Unit test: `TestCherryPickToTarget_ConflictCleanup`

File: `cmd/bd/branch_messaging_test.go` (new file)

Requires Dolt server (uses `newTestStoreIsolatedDB` — needs isolated DB since test creates branches and cherry-picks).

**Test scenario:**
1. Create a test store with isolated DB, create an issue on main, commit
2. Register a branch with `merge-with-branch` strategy (creates Dolt branch via `RegisterBranch`)
3. On the branch: modify the same issue to create a conflicting state, commit
4. On main: modify the same issue differently, commit — get the commit hash
5. Open a merge connection (`openMergeConnection`), call `cherryPickToTarget` to cherry-pick main's commit onto the branch
6. **Assert:** cherry-pick returns an error containing "conflict"
7. **Assert:** after the error, the branch working set is clean (verify via `SELECT * FROM dolt_status` returning no dirty tables)

This test verifies that `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')` actually cleans up, rather than leaving dirty state.

### Verification of correct usage in `branch_strategy.go`

No test needed — `mergeOnClose` at line 70 correctly uses `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` after `DOLT_MERGE()`. Just a manual review confirmation.

## Implementation Plan

1. **Fix the bug** — `cmd/bd/branch_messaging.go:84`: change `DOLT_MERGE('--abort')` to `DOLT_CHERRY_PICK('--abort')`
2. **Write test** — `cmd/bd/branch_messaging_test.go`:
   - `TestCherryPickToTarget_ConflictCleanup`: Uses `newTestStoreIsolatedDB` + `openMergeConnection` to exercise the real cherry-pick conflict path against Dolt
   - Build tag: `//go:build cgo` (matches existing test files)
   - Skips if test Dolt server not available
3. **Verify** — Run `go test ./cmd/bd/ -run TestCherryPickToTarget -v` against test Dolt server

## References

- Dolt SQL Procedures docs: https://docs.dolthub.com/sql-reference/version-control/dolt-sql-procedures
- Introduced in commit 9bdc176c (this PR branch)
- Compare with correct usage in `branch_strategy.go:70`

## Worktree

Work branch: bd-vlu.1.18--refactor (worktree at bd-vlu.1.18--refactor/)

## Key requirements (from review)

1. **branch_strategy.enabled is cruft** — it should not exist or be referenced anywhere. branch_strategy is enabled if ANY branch_strategy.* key is found in config.yaml. IsBranchStrategyEnabled() already does this correctly (presence-based). Verify no explicit "enabled" key exists in code, config templates, or docs.

2. **No managed branching strategies until Phase 2** — beads should not disallow ad hoc branching, but bd reset must work independently of any branching strategy. Remove all strategy selection, registration, and merge machinery.

3. **Strategy prompt must be removed** — the A/B/C strategy selection prompt (maybePromptBranchStrategy) is Phase 2. The only prompt in Phase 1 is the reset prompt (§8.3): "Dolt is behind git. Reset Dolt? [Y/n]". Remove and re-implement in Phase 2.

4. **No git checkout hooks unless related to bd reset** — the post-checkout hook (runPostCheckoutHook) is already a stub (runs chained hooks, returns 0) and does not call Phase 2 code. It stays. - writeBeadsRefs / readBeadsRefs / cleanupStaleBeadsRefs (dolt_head.go)

Create/extend post-checkout git hook (gist §4.4, §6.4, §6.5). Two responsibilities:

(1) Beads ref sync: On branch switch, read .beads/HEAD to determine expected Dolt branch, compare .beads/refs/heads/<branch> hash against current Dolt state. On mismatch, prompt Reset/Keep. Safety guards: skip during rebase (check rebase-merge/rebase-apply dirs), skip during stash (check third arg=0), skip file-level checkouts.

(2) Branch strategy prompt: On new branch creation, detect unregistered Dolt branch, prompt for merge strategy [A/B/C], register in beads_branches.

Non-interactive support via BD_MERGE_STRATEGY and BD_AUTO_SYNC env vars.

description:
Add Go tests for the beads refs feature (Phase 1). Currently zero automated test coverage — all validation was manual.

## Summary

Automated tests for writeBeadsRefs(), readBeadsRefs(), and the checkBeadsRefSync() control flow (in PersistentPreRun). These are the core functions enabling git-Dolt time-travel correspondence. Tests should be unit-level where possible (no Dolt server needed for ref file I/O) and use existing test patterns (newTestStoreWithPrefix, t.TempDir()).

## Functional Requirements

1. **Ref file I/O** — writeBeadsRefs creates correct file structure; readBeadsRefs round-trips correctly
2. **Edge cases** — missing files, malformed HEAD content, missing refs dir, empty hash
3. **PersistentPreRun control flow** — checkBeadsRefSync skips when refs match, skips for commands in skip list
4. **Settings behavior** — all four branch_strategy combinations:
   - prompt=false, reset=false: silent (no action)
   - prompt=false, reset=true: auto-reset, stderr message
   - prompt=true, reset=false: prompt [y/N]
   - prompt=true, reset=true: prompt [Y/n]
5. **Mismatch detection and reset correctness** — checkBeadsRefSync detects hash mismatch and triggers DOLT_RESET. This is the highest-value, highest-risk test. DOLT_RESET('--hard', hash) resets ALL Dolt-managed tables to the state at that commit. The test MUST verify:
   - **Issues created AFTER the checkpoint are gone**
   - **Issues that existed AT the checkpoint are preserved** with correct fields
   - **Dependencies/labels/comments created after checkpoint are gone**
   - **Pre-checkpoint dependencies/labels/comments are preserved**
   - **Round-trip integrity** — reset backward then forward, no corruption or duplication
   - **Ref files match after reset** — contains the hash we reset TO
   - Test uses branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git=true code path (non-interactive)
6. **No math references** — grep guard ensuring no ~/Projects/math or math-* issue IDs leaked into the codebase

## Technical Direction

- File: cmd/bd/dolt_head_test.go (ref I/O tests, no DB needed)
- File: cmd/bd/hooks_checkout_test.go or new cmd/bd/sync_test.go (mismatch detection, needs test store)
- Pattern: match existing test style (//go:build cgo, package main, newTestStoreWithPrefix)
- readBeadsRefs/writeBeadsRefs are pure I/O — testable with t.TempDir() alone
- checkBeadsRefSync needs a real DoltStore for the reset path; use newTestStoreWithPrefix

## Test Plan

### Unit Tests (cmd/bd/dolt_head_test.go) — no Dolt needed

**readBeadsRefs**:
- Happy path: valid HEAD + valid ref → returns (hash, branch)
- Missing HEAD file → ("", "")
- Malformed HEAD (no "ref: refs/heads/" prefix) → ("", "")
- HEAD valid but ref file missing → ("", branch)
- Trailing whitespace/newlines in files → trimmed correctly
- Branch name with slashes (e.g., "feature/foo") → handled

**writeBeadsRefs round-trip**:
- Write then read → same hash and branch returned
- Creates refs/heads/ directory if missing
- Overwrites existing ref file on update
- Nil or closed store → returns without error (no-op)

### Unit Tests (cmd/bd/hooks_checkout_test.go) — no Dolt needed

**runPostCheckoutHook arg filtering**:
- args with flag=1 → calls postCheckoutBeadsSync (via side effect detection)
- args with flag=0 → returns 0 without sync
- args too short (<3) → returns 0 without sync

**isRebaseInProgress**:
- No sentinel dirs → false
- .git/rebase-merge exists → true
- .git/rebase-apply exists → true
- Both exist → true

### Integration Tests (cmd/bd/hooks_checkout_test.go) — needs Dolt

**TestPostCheckoutResetCorrectness** (highest-value test):
Setup:
1. Create store, create issue A with label, dependency, and comment → Dolt auto-commit → record hash as CHECKPOINT
2. Create issue B with its own label, dependency, and comment → Dolt auto-commit → record hash as LATEST
3. Write .beads/refs with CHECKPOINT hash (simulating git checkout to older commit)

Reset backward (LATEST → CHECKPOINT):
4. Set BD_AUTO_SYNC=1, call postCheckoutBeadsSync()
5. Assert issue B is GONE (query returns not-found)
6. Assert issue A is PRESENT with correct title, status, priority, type, description
7. Assert issue A's label, dependency, and comment are PRESENT
8. Assert issue B's label, dependency, and comment are GONE
9. Assert .beads/refs/heads/<branch> contains CHECKPOINT hash

Reset forward (CHECKPOINT → LATEST):
10. Write .beads/refs with LATEST hash, call postCheckoutBeadsSync() again
11. Assert BOTH issues A and B are present with all original fields intact
12. Assert ALL labels, dependencies, and comments for both issues are present
13. Assert no duplicate rows (count matches expected)
14. Assert .beads/refs/heads/<branch> contains LATEST hash

**TestPostCheckoutBeadsSyncInSync**:
- Write refs matching current Dolt hash → no reset occurs, Dolt HEAD unchanged

**TestPostCheckoutBeadsSyncNoRefs**:
- No ref files present → skip gracefully (no panic, no reset)

### Static Guard (cmd/bd/dolt_head_test.go)

**No math test bed references**:
- Grep all .go files for "math-" issue ID patterns (math-ryv, math-r62, etc.)
- Grep all .go files for "Projects/math" path references
- Test fails if any matches found (excluding test file itself)

### Run command
go test -v -run TestBeadsRefs ./cmd/bd/
go test -v -run TestPostCheckout ./cmd/bd/
go test -v -run TestNoMathReferences ./cmd/bd/

description:
## Summary

Move the Dolt mismatch detection and reset logic from `postCheckoutBeadsSync()` (called in the post-checkout git hook) to `PersistentPreRun` (the setup phase of every `bd` command). Implement `branch_strategy.*` config settings that control mismatch behavior.

## Why

The current design pairs `git checkout` (non-destructive, detached HEAD) with `DOLT_RESET('--hard')` (destructive, moves branch pointer). These are semantically different operations. Additionally, the hook runs in a subprocess with a fresh Dolt connection that has lifecycle issues — the reset doesn't persist reliably.

Moving to PersistentPreRun:
1. **Correct semantic pairing**: Detection fires after `git reset` (destructive) when the user runs the next `bd` command. Both git reset and Dolt reset move branch pointers — matching semantics.
2. **Reliable store context**: PersistentPreRun already initializes the Dolt store properly. No `ensureStoreActive()` workaround needed.
3. **No false positives from checkout**: `git checkout <old-commit>` (detached HEAD) doesn't automatically trigger a destructive Dolt reset.

## Configuration

Four settings under the `branch_strategy` namespace (stored via `bd config`):

```
branch_strategy.default_strategy                  # stay-on-main | merge-with-branch | merge-on-close
branch_strategy.prompt                            # true/false — prompt on new branch, checkout, reset
branch_strategy.defaults.checkout_dolt_with_git   # true/false — git checkout triggers Dolt checkout
branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git      # true/false — git reset triggers Dolt reset
```

All default to `false` (or `stay-on-main` for default_strategy).

**Behavior on mismatch:**

| prompt | defaults.reset_dolt_with_git | On git reset mismatch |
|--------|----------------------------|----------------------|
| false  | false                      | **Silent** (default). Mismatch logged to debug, no action. |
| false  | true                       | **Auto-reset**. Dolt resets silently. Message to stderr. |
| true   | false                      | **Prompt [y/N]**. Default is No (keep current state). |
| true   | true                       | **Prompt [Y/n]**. Default is Yes (reset). |

**Phase 1 ships with all defaults false (silent mode).** Zero user-visible behavior changes. Users opt in via settings. Phase 2 changes `branch_strategy.prompt` default to `true`.

No environment variable overrides. CI/agents use `bd config set` instead.

## Prompt Design

```
beads: Reset Beads to match Git history?
  (Dolt state at Git commit <hash> was previously <ref-hash>.)

  Yes, DOLT_RESET --hard <ref-hash>
  No, preserve Dolt state at <current-hash>

  Note: If you preserve Dolt state at <current-hash>, you can manually
  DOLT_RESET --hard <ref-hash> later. If you reset now, Dolt state at
  <current-hash> is still recoverable by hash (DOLT_RESET --hard <current-hash>)
  but no branch will point to it.

Choice [y/N]:
```

Default action reflects `defaults.reset_dolt_with_git`. Skipped in non-TTY environments (warns to stderr).

## Changes Required

1. **`cmd/bd/main.go` PersistentPreRun**: After store initialization, call a new `checkBeadsRefSync()` function that reads ref files, compares with current Dolt state, and takes action based on `branch_strategy.*` settings.
2. **`cmd/bd/config.go`** (or equivalent): Register `branch_strategy.prompt`, `branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git`, `branch_strategy.defaults.checkout_dolt_with_git`, and `branch_strategy.default_strategy` config keys.
3. **`cmd/bd/hooks.go`**: Remove `postCheckoutBeadsSync()` from `runPostCheckoutHook()`. The post-checkout hook retains rebase/stash guards and the branch strategy prompt (Phase 2+), but no longer does mismatch detection or Dolt resets.
4. **Skip list**: The sync check should skip for commands where it doesn't make sense (e.g., `hooks`, `init`, `version`, `help`, `doctor`, `config`).
5. **Tests**: Update `hooks_checkout_test.go` to test the new location and all four settings combinations.

## Acceptance Criteria

- With default settings (all false): `git reset --hard HEAD~1` followed by `bd list` produces NO prompt, NO reset, NO visible output
- `bd config set branch_strategy.prompt true`: mismatch triggers prompt with [y/N]
- `bd config set branch_strategy.defaults.reset_dolt_with_git true` (prompt false): mismatch auto-resets silently, message to stderr
- Both true: prompt shows [Y/n] (default is reset)
- Default (press Enter) on [y/N] prompt keeps current Dolt state
- Typing `y` on prompt syncs Dolt to the ref hash
- No prompt when refs match current Dolt state
- No prompt for commands in the skip list
- Post-checkout hook no longer calls `postCheckoutBeadsSync()`
notes:
## Design Rationale (2026-03-06)

The original Phase 1 design put mismatch detection in the post-checkout git hook. This was wrong for two reasons:

1. **Semantic mismatch**: The post-checkout hook pairs `git checkout` (non-destructive, detached HEAD) with `DOLT_RESET('--hard')` (destructive, moves branch pointer). These are fundamentally different operations. Git checkout preserves all history; Dolt reset moves the branch pointer.

2. **Lifecycle issues**: The hook runs `bd` as a subprocess with a fresh Dolt connection via `ensureStoreActive()`. The reset didn't persist reliably — "following the prompt to reset has no effect" was the observed bug.

### Resolution

Split time-travel into two operations matching git's own semantics:
- **Reset** (Phase 1): `git reset` ↔ `DOLT_RESET`. Both destructive, both move branch pointer. Detection in PersistentPreRun.
- **Checkout** (Phase 2): `git checkout` ↔ temporary Dolt branch. Both non-destructive. Requires branch management infrastructure from Strategy B.

This simplifies Phase 1 (no detached HEAD handling), makes it correct (matching semantics), and keeps it mergeable (reset is self-contained, checkout depends on Phase 2 infrastructure).
title: Move mismatch detection from post-checkout hook to PersistentPreRun

description:
## Summary

Beads refs are off by default. If no branch_strategy.* keys exist in config.yaml, no ref files are generated — equivalent to Strategy A without history tracking.

When any branch_strategy.* key is uncommented in config.yaml, the feature activates: ref files are written, mismatch detection runs, and bd reset/check-refs perform sync checks.

## Detection

IsBranchStrategyEnabled() in internal/config/config.go checks if any branch_strategy.* key is set via v.GetStringMap("branch_strategy"). Commented-out keys are invisible to viper.

## Guard points (4 places)

1. writeBeadsRefs() in dolt_head.go — early return if not enabled
2. checkBeadsRefSync() in dolt_head.go — early return if not enabled
3. bd reset Run in git_reset.go — skip sync check if not enabled (still wraps git reset)
4. bd check-refs Run in git_reset.go — print "refs disabled" to stderr if not enabled

## Cleanup on disable

When checkBeadsRefSync detects refs disabled but .beads/HEAD exists:
- git rm --cached the ref files (stage deletion)
- Print warning to stderr with suggested commit message
- Only trigger once (check file existence)

## Config template update

Update init_templates.go branch_strategy comments to explain:
1. Off by default, uncomment to enable
2. Which settings are active now vs forthcoming (checkout_dolt_with_git not yet supported)
3. Off = Strategy A without history tracking. On + Strategy A = history tracking enabling bd reset
4. Strategy A is the only supported strategy; B and C are forthcoming

## Tests

- IsBranchStrategyEnabled() returns false when no keys set, true when any key set
- writeBeadsRefs is a no-op when disabled
- checkBeadsRefSync is a no-op when disabled
- Cleanup stages deletion when ref files exist but feature disabled
title: Add branch_strategy.enabled setting to disable ref generation entirely

Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running with Dolt server

## Test 1: Non-conflicting concurrent updates (cell-level merge)
1. bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy merge-on-close
2. git checkout -b test/conflict-a (post-checkout hook registers)
3. git checkout main && git checkout -b test/conflict-b (post-checkout hook registers)
4. From test/conflict-a: bd create --title="Conflict test issue" --type=task --priority=1
5. bd close <id> --reason="merge to main" (triggers auto-merge)
6. From test/conflict-b: bd update <id> --notes="Adding notes from branch B"
7. bd close <id-of-another-issue> --reason="merge to main"
8. Verify: both updates on main (cell-level merge, no conflict — different fields)

## Test 2: Conflicting updates (same cell)
1. From test/conflict-a: bd create --title="Same cell conflict" --type=task --priority=0
2. bd close <id> --reason="merge" (triggers auto-merge, gets to main)
3. From test/conflict-b: bd update <same-id> --priority=3
4. bd close <another-id> --reason="trigger merge"
5. Observe: merge conflict on priority field — should see warning in stderr
6. Verify: branch B's close still succeeds (work continues), merge skipped with warning

## Test 3: Self-healing retry
1. From test/conflict-b: make another bd update (any field)
2. bd close <yet-another-id> --reason="retry merge"
3. Observe: retry attempt — does it merge cleanly now?

## Cleanup
git checkout main && git branch -D test/conflict-a test/conflict-b
bd config set branch_strategy.default_strategy stay-on-main

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Non-conflicting concurrent updates merge cleanly (cell-level)
- [ ] True conflicts (same cell) abort gracefully with warning
- [ ] Work continues on branch after failed merge
- [ ] Retry on next close attempts merge again
- [ ] Warning message clear enough for debugging
title: [Test] Validate conflict resolution — manufacture and resolve merge conflicts

description: Implement message cherry-pick mechanism (§6.1-6.6). When bd create -t message or message update occurs: (1) Commit message on current branch, capture commit hash. (2) Cherry-pick to all active branches via dedicated delivery connection — iterate beads_branches WHERE status='active' AND branch_name \!= current. Messages are append-only inserts with hash-based IDs, should apply cleanly. On cherry-pick failure: log warning, continue (clean failure, no dirty state). Dolt commit message records source branch. Handle concurrency (§6.5): O(N) cherry-picks per message, ~50-100ms at 3-5 polecats.
title: Implement cross-branch message delivery via cherry-pick

description:
UX polish and shell integration for branch_strategy, informed by real-world usage of Phases 1–4.

## What Might Ship

- Shell integration for transparent `git reset` interception (shell function, git wrapper script, per-project vs global scope)
- UX refinements informed by early usage of Phases 1–4
- Graduation of experimental features to stable defaults

## UX Decisions Deferred to Phase 5

- Is `branch_strategy.prompt` defaulting to `true` (since Phase 1) the right call? Phase 5 may adjust based on feedback.
- Should `bd reset` be the only documented time-travel interface, or should shell integration be the recommended path?
- Are there other git operations (e.g., `git stash pop`, `git cherry-pick`) that need similar wrapping?
- Should Phases 1–4 features that shipped as "experimental" / "off by default" graduate to stable / on by default?

## Design Principle

Keep features experimental and off by default through Phase 4. Phase 5 reviews real usage data and makes final calls on defaults, discoverability, and which integrations are worth the complexity.

## Shell Integration Options (evaluate based on Phase 1–4 feedback)

1. **Shell function** — users add a `git()` wrapper to `.bashrc`/`.zshrc` that calls `bd check-refs` after reset.
2. **Git wrapper script** — a `git` script placed in `$PATH` that delegates to the real binary but intercepts reset.
3. **Per-project vs global** — decide scope based on user feedback.

## Gate

Shell integration installs/removes cleanly across bash, zsh, and fish. No regressions in existing `bd reset` / `bd check-refs` behavior. UX decisions documented as ADRs with rationale from usage data.

See §14.5 in Gist.BranchingStrategies.md for full context.
title: Phase 5: UX Polish and Shell Integration

description:
## Problem

`bd config set branch_strategy.prompt true` appends the key as a flat line at the end of config.yaml, far from the commented-out `branch_strategy` template section where users would expect to find it. The current `updateYamlKey()` function in `internal/config/yaml_config.go` does line-by-line regex matching — it cannot understand nested YAML structure or locate a child key under a commented parent section.

## Use Case

The `bd init` template generates config.yaml with well-documented commented sections:

```yaml
# branch_strategy:
#   prompt: true
#   defaults:
#     reset_dolt_with_git: false
```

When a user runs `bd config set branch_strategy.prompt true`, the expected result is the key appearing near its documentation — either uncommenting the existing line or inserting under the parent. Instead it lands at EOF:

```yaml
# ... 90 lines of template ...
branch_strategy.prompt: true
```

Viper reads both forms identically, so this is a UX/discoverability issue, not a correctness bug.

## Options Evaluated

### Option 1: `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` Node API (already a dependency)

yaml.v3 Node type has HeadComment, LineComment, and FootComment fields. Decode to yaml.Node tree, surgically modify nodes, re-encode. **Caveat**: re-encoding does not perfectly preserve original formatting — it re-renders "pleasantly" but may change whitespace, quoting style, blank lines. Good enough for most config files, but not byte-for-byte round-trip.

### Option 2: `github.com/vmware-labs/go-yaml-edit` (active)

The vmware-archive version is dead, but vmware-labs/go-yaml-edit appears maintained. Implements `golang.org/x/text/transform.Transformer` for surgical in-place byte-stream edits — preserves formatting, indentation, quoting, and comments by only touching the bytes at the edit site. Built on top of yaml.v3 Node API. This is the closest purpose-built solution.

### Option 3: `github.com/goccy/go-yaml`

Claims reversible transformation preserving anchors, aliases, and comments. Passes 60+ more YAML test suite cases than yaml.v3. Written from scratch. Would be a new dependency and a different API surface from what Viper uses internally.

### Option 4: Stay with line-based regex (current approach)

The current updateYamlKey works for flat keys. For nested keys, the template comments serve as docs and bd config set appends flat keys at the bottom. Viper reads both forms identically. Functional but not great UX.

## Assessment

vmware-labs/go-yaml-edit is the most interesting — purpose-built for this exact problem. The yaml.v3 Node API could also work but requires more custom code and imperfect formatting preservation. Both would need updateYamlKey rewritten to understand nested structure and handle the "uncomment parent section" case.

## References

- [vmware-labs/go-yaml-edit on pkg.go.dev](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/vmware-labs/go-yaml-edit)
- [go-yaml/yaml issue #709 — preserve comments](https://github.com/go-yaml/yaml/issues/709)
- [goccy/go-yaml on GitHub](https://github.com/goccy/go-yaml)
- [gopkg.in/yaml.v3 docs](https://pkg.go.dev/gopkg.in/yaml.v3)
- Current implementation: `internal/config/yaml_config.go:updateYamlKey()`
title: Improve YAML writer: write config near corresponding comments
